problem: The ticket describes an Ego Agent concept requiring CLI-level support for agent-to-system interaction. The CLI currently provides comment posting/listing (hlx comments) and production inspection (hlx inspect) as a stateless command executor for agents. It is the mechanism through which in-sandbox agents post feedback and read runtime data, making it a critical interface layer for the ego agent's operational capabilities.

files.why: CLI command to post a comment to a ticket. Used by in-sandbox agents to post @Helix-tagged comments. Resolves ticket ID from --ticket flag or HELIX_TICKET_ID env var.
CLI command to list comments with --helix-only and --since filters. Used by agents to read discussion context.
Comment command router. Dispatches to list/post subcommands.
Execute SQL queries against production databases. Relevant to ego agent's ability to diagnose production issues.
Query production logs. Relevant to ego agent's deploy failure diagnosis capability.
Call production API endpoints. Relevant to ego agent's runtime inspection capabilities.
List available repositories and their inspection types (DATABASE, LOGS, API).
HTTP client with 30s timeout, 3 retries with exponential backoff. Centralized resilience for all CLI operations.
Configuration loading from env vars (HELIX_API_KEY, HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN, HELIX_URL) or ~/.hlx/config.json.
CLI entry point. Routes to login, inspect, or comments commands.
Zero runtime dependencies, Node >=18. Build: tsc. Typecheck: tsc --noEmit.

facts: The CLI is a stateless command executor: agents provide context (ticket ID, SQL, log queries), CLI makes HTTP requests, returns JSON, exits.
Comment commands: hlx comments list (with --helix-only, --since filters) and hlx comments post (positional message arg).
Inspect commands: hlx inspect db (SQL), hlx inspect logs (with --limit), hlx inspect api (HTTP proxy), hlx inspect repos (list available).
Auth supports both Bearer token and API key (hxi_ prefix detection). Config from env vars or ~/.hlx/config.json.
HTTP layer has 30s timeout, 3 retries with exponential backoff (2s base + jitter), retryable on 429/5xx.
Zero runtime dependencies - uses only Node.js built-in fetch and standard library.
The CLI does NOT clone repositories, manage sandboxes, or orchestrate runs. It is a thin RPC interface.
HELIX_TICKET_ID env var provides implicit ticket context for comments commands inside sandbox agents.
unknowns: No CLI command exists for listing or managing multiple tickets (only per-ticket comment interaction). Unknown if a grand ego agent would need CLI-level multi-ticket commands.
No CLI command for APL inspection or modification. Unknown if agents need CLI access to APL artifacts.
No CLI command for viewing run status or triggering reruns. These operations go through the web API directly.
Unknown if the CLI would need to support real-time streaming (SSE/WebSocket) for live ego agent interaction, or if comment polling is sufficient.
